We need to complete the proof for `sSup_eq_top'`. Let's think about how to approach this.

We need to show that `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤`.

We know from `hB` that for each `N ∈ D`, `N = sSup (B N hN)`. So each element `N` in `D` is the supremum of `B N hN`. Since `D` is a direct sum decomposition, `sSup D = ⊤`. 

Now, `D ⊆ ⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN` because for any `N ∈ D`, `N = sSup (B N hN)`, so `N` is in the union. Therefore, `sSup D ≤ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`. Since `sSup D = ⊤`, we get `⊤ ≤ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`.

For the reverse inequality, let `m ∈ M`. Since `D` is a direct sum decomposition, `m ∈ sSup D`. So `m ∈ N` for some `N ∈ D`. By `hB`, `N = sSup (B N hN)`. So `m ∈ sSup (B N hN)`. Since `B N hN ⊆ ⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN`, we have `m ∈ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`. Thus, `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤`.

import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · sorry
  sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ∀ A : I, ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← hf' A.prop]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ≤ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ≤ A ∧ N.val ≤ B → A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ⟨h_le, h_neq⟩
  have h_neq' : A.val ≠ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ≤ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ⟨h_mem⟩
      · exact (h_mem ▸ A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ≤ A.val ⊓ B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ≤ (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ▸ N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N₁ N₂ : J) (h_le₁ : N₂.val ≤ RefinementMap I J h N₁) :
    RefinementMap I J h N₂ = RefinementMap I J h N₁ := by
  have h_le₂ : N₂.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N₂) := RefinementMapLE I J h N₂
  exact (UniqueGE I J N₂ (RefinementMap I J h N₁) (RefinementMap I J h N₂) (⟨h_le₁, h_le₂⟩)).symm

/-- If `N ≤ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)

  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ∀ A : I, ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← hf' A.prop]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ≤ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ≤ A ∧ N.val ≤ B → A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ⟨h_le, h_neq⟩
  have h_neq' : A.val ≠ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ≤ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ⟨h_mem⟩
      · exact (h_mem ▸ A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ≤ A.val ⊓ B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ≤ (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ▸ N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`. Then
`RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : Direct

`RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N₁ N₂ : J) (h_le₁ : N₂.val ≤ RefinementMap I J h N₁) :
    RefinementMap I J h N₂ = RefinementMap I J h N₁ := by
  have h_le₂ : N₂.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N₂) := RefinementMapLE I J h N₂
  exact (UniqueGE I J N₂ (RefinementMap I J h N₁) (RefinementMap I J h N₂) (⟨h_le₁, h_le₂⟩)).symm

/-- If `N ≤ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ≤ A → (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (⟨h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N⟩)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ≤ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D₁ ≤ D₂` if
and only if `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D₁ D₂ := IsRefinement D₁ D₂
  --D₁ ≤ D₂ iff D₁ refines D₂.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I₁ I₂ I₃ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h23 h_mem) with ⟨C, h_sub, h_eq⟩
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := ⨆ (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    · apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    · have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        · intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ∃ (c : C), a ∈ (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ⟨c_a, h_ca⟩
          have h_le : a ≤ c_a := by rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ⟨c, h_mem_c⟩
        have h_le_c : c ≤ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by
          rw [← hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ∈ A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ≤ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ∀ N ∈ J, N ∈ I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ⟨h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I⟩
      let N' : J := ⟨N, h_N_in_J⟩
      have h_A : ∃ A : I, N ≤ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ⟨A, h_N_le_A⟩
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let h_B₁ := hf' N'.prop
      let h_B₂ := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B₁
      have h_mem : A.val ∈ f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B₂ h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ≤ A.val := h_B₁ ▸ h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [← h_B₁] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ≤ N := by
        simpa [← h_B₁] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ∈ I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ▸ A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ∀ N ∈ I, N ∈ J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `S` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ∀ N ∈ D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ∀ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ∀ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ∀ N hN, ⊥ ∉ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by
    sorry
```

The goal is to prove `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤`.

We have a direct sum decomposition `D` of `M` and for each `N ∈ D`, a set `B N hN` such that:
1. `N = sSup (B N hN)`
2. `sSupIndep (B N hN)`
3. `⊥ ∉ B N hN`

We need to show that the set `⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN` has supremum `⊤`.

Let's think of how to approach this. The union is a set of submodules. The supremum of a set of submodules is the sum of all the submodules. So we need to show that the sum of all submodules in the union equals `M`.

But we have a direct sum decomposition `D` of `M`, so `sSup D = ⊤`. The union is a different set. How does it relate to `D`?

Wait, perhaps the key is to use the fact that `N = sSup (B N hN)` for all `N ∈ D`.

So, for each `N ∈ D`, we have `N = sSup (B N hN)`. Therefore, `N` is the sum of the submodules in `B N hN`.

If we take the union of all `B N hN` for `N ∈ D`, then the supremum of the union would be the sum of all these submodules.

But since `D` is a direct sum decomposition, `sSup D = ⊤`. The union is a different set, but its supremum should also be `⊤`.

Let's write the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decompositon of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · sorry
  sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨A, h_A, h_le⟩
    exact h_le.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨A, h_A, h_le⟩
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_A h_le
    have h_le_subset : ∀ A : I, ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← hf' A.prop]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C, h_le⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ≤ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ≤ A ∧ N.val ≤ B → A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ⟨h_le, h_neq⟩
  have h_neq' : A.val ≠ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ≤ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ⟨h_mem⟩
      · exact (h_mem ▸ A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ≤ A.val ⊓ B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ≤ (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ▸ N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[

`RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N₁ N₂ : J) (h_le₁ : N₂.val ≤ RefinementMap I J h N₁) :
    RefinementMap I J h N₂ = RefinementMap I J h N₁ := by
  have h_le₂ : N₂.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N₂) := RefinementMapLE I J h N₂
  exact (UniqueGE I J N₂ (RefinementMap I J h N₁) (RefinementMap I J h N₂) (⟨h_le₁, h_le₂⟩)).symm

/-- If `N ≤ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ≤ A → (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (⟨h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N⟩)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ≤ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D₁ ≤ D₂` if
and only if `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D₁ D₂ := IsRefinement D₁ D₂
  --D₁ ≤ D₂ iff D₁ refines D₂.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I₁ I₂ I₃ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h23 h_mem) with ⟨C, h_sub, h_eq⟩
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := ⨆ (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    · apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    · have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        · intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ∃ (c : C), a ∈ (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ⟨c_a, h_ca⟩
          have h_le : a ≤ c_a := by rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ⟨c, h_mem_c⟩
        have h_le_c : c ≤ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by
          rw [← hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ∈ A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ≤ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ∀ N ∈ J, N ∈ I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ⟨h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I⟩
      let N' : J := ⟨N, h_N_in_J⟩
      have h_A : ∃ A : I, N ≤ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ⟨A, h_N_le_A⟩
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let h_B₁ := hf' N'.prop
      let h_B₂ := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B₁
      have h_mem : A.val ∈ f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B₂ h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ≤ A.val := h_B₁ ▸ h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [← h_B₁] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ≤ N := by
        simpa [← h_B₁] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ∈ I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ▸ A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ∀ N ∈ I, N ∈ J := by
      sorry
    aesop

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `S` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ∀ N ∈ D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ∀ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ∀ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ∀ N hN, ⊥ ∉ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by
    sorry
```

We need to complete the proof of the theorem `refinement` which states that given a direct sum decomposition `D` of a persistence module `M` and a way to decompose each submodule in `D` into a direct sum of submodules, the resulting set of submodules forms a new direct sum decomposition of `M`.

The theorem statement is:
```tactics
theorem refinement (B : ∀ N ∈ D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ∀ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ∀ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ∀ N hN, ⊥ ∉ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by
    sorry
```

We need to prove three things to show that `⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN` is a direct sum decomposition of `M`:
1. `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN) = ⊤`
2. `sSupIndep (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN)`
3. `¬ (⊥ ∈ ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN)`

Let's think about how to prove each of these.

1. **Proving `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN) = ⊤`**:
   - We need to show that the supremum of all submodules in the union is the entire module `M`.
   - We know that `D` is a direct sum decomposition, so `sSup D = ⊤`.
   - For each `N ∈ D`, we have `N = sSup (B N hN)` by `hB`.
   - So, we need to show that `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN) = sSup D`.
   - This follows from the fact that `sSup` preserves unions when the sets are nested. Since each `B N hN` is a set of submodules of `N`, and `N` itself is in `D`, we have `sSup (B N hN) = N`. The union of these `N`'s is exactly `D`. So `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN) = sSup D = ⊤`.

2. **Proving `sSupIndep (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN)`**:
   - We need to show that the set of submodules is linearly independent.
   - We know that `D` is linearly independent.
   - For each `N ∈ D`, the set `B N hN` is linearly independent (by `hB'`).
   - We need to show that if `N₁, ..., Nₖ` are distinct submodules from the union, they are linearly independent.
   - Since they come from different `N ∈ D` (which are linearly independent) and `B N hN` is linearly independent for each `N`, the whole set is linearly independent.

3. **Proving `¬ (⊥ ∈ ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN)`**:
   - We need to show that the zero submodule `⊥` is not in the union.
   - We know that `D` does not contain `⊥` (`not_bot_mem'`).
   - For each `N ∈ D`, `B N hN` does not contain `⊥` (by `hB''`).
   - So, the union cannot contain `⊥`.

Let's complete the Lean proof:

```tactics

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)

We need to prove `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤`.

Let's think about how to prove this. We need to show that the supremum of all the submodules in our collection is equal to the top element `⊤`.

We can use the fact that `D` is a direct sum decomposition, which means `sSup D = ⊤`. But `D` itself is a set of submodules. So we need to relate the `sSup` of the union of these sets `B N hN` to `sSup D`.

Since `D` is a direct sum decomposition, we have `sSup D = ⊤`. This means that the supremum of all submodules in `D` is the entire module `M`.

Now, let's think about the relationship between `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)` and `sSup D`.

Each element `N ∈ D` is equal to `sSup (B N hN)` by `hB`. So, `N ⊆ sSup (B N hN)` for all `N ∈ D`.

Therefore, `sSup (B N hN)` is a set of submodules whose supremum is `N`.

Now, we need to show that the supremum of all these submodules is `⊤`.

Let's try to prove this directly. We need to show that any element `m ∈ M` is in `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`.

So, for any `m ∈ M`, we need to show that `m ∈ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`.

This means that `m` is in the union, so there exists some `N ∈ D` and `p ∈ B N hN` such that `m ∈ p`.

But `p ∈ B N hN` means `p ≤ N` (since `B N hN` is a set of submodules, and `p` is a submodule). So `m ∈ p ≤ N`.

Now, since `D` is a direct sum decomposition, `sSup D = ⊤`. So, for any `m ∈ M`, there exists some `N ∈ D` such that `m ∈ N`. This is the definition of `sSup D = ⊤`.

But we need to show that for any `m ∈ M`, `m` is in `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`.

Let's think about this carefully. We know that `D` is a direct sum decomposition, so `sSup D = ⊤`. This means that for any `m ∈ M`, there is some `N ∈ D` such that `m ∈ N`. This is the definition of `sSup D = ⊤`.

Now, if we take such an `N`, then by `hB`, we have `N = sSup (B N hN)`. So, `m ∈ N = sSup (B N hN)`. This means `m ∈ sSup (B N hN)`.

But `sSup (B N hN)` is a submodule. So, `m ∈ sSup (B N hN)` means `m` is in the union.

Therefore, `m ∈ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`.

So, we need to show that `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤`.

We can try to prove this by showing that any element `m ∈ M` is in the `sSup` of the union.

  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=

`RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N₁ N₂ : J) (h_le₁ : N₂.val ≤ RefinementMap I J h N₁) :
    RefinementMap I J h N₂ = RefinementMap I J h N₁ := by
  have h_le₂ : N₂.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N₂) := RefinementMapLE I J h N₂
  exact (UniqueGE I J N₂ (RefinementMap I J h N₁) (RefinementMap I J h N₂) (⟨h_le₁, h_le₂⟩)).symm

/-- If `N ≤ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ≤ A → (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (⟨h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N⟩)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ≤ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D₁ ≤ D₂` if
and only if `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D₁ D₂ := IsRefinement D₁ D₂
  --D₁ ≤ D₂ iff D₁ refines D₂.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I₁ I₂ I₃ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h23 h_mem) with ⟨C, h_sub, h_eq⟩
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := ⨆ (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    · apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    · have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        · intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ∃ (c : C), a ∈ (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ⟨c_a, h_ca⟩
          have h_le : a ≤ c_a := by rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca

        let c' : C := ⟨c, h_mem_c⟩
        have h_le_c : c ≤ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by

            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ∀ N ∈ J, N ∈ I := by

The proof is incomplete, and we need to fill in the `sorry` placeholders.
The main goal is to prove `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤`.

To prove this, we need to show two things:
1. `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) ≥ ⊤`
2. `sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) ≤ ⊤`

For the second part (`≤ ⊤`), it's trivial because `sSup S ≤ ⊤` for any set `S`.
For the first part (`≥ ⊤`), we need to show that every submodule `P` of `M` is contained in the sSup of the set. That is, for any submodule `P`, we need `P ≤ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`.

To show this, let `P` be an arbitrary submodule of `M`. Since `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M`, we have `sSup D = ⊤`. This means `P ≤ sSup D`. By the definition of `sSup`, this implies `P ≤ N` for some `N ∈ D`.

Let `N₀` be such a submodule in `D`. Since `B N₀ (hN₀)` is a direct sum decomposition of `N₀` (by `hB`, `hB'`, `hB''`), we have `sSup (B N₀ hN₀) = N₀`. Therefore, any submodule `P'` of `N₀` is contained in `sSup (B N₀ hN₀)`.

Since `P ≤ N₀`, we can write `P` as `sSup P` (if `P ≠ ⊥`) or `P = ⊥` (if `P = ⊥`). In either case, `P` is a submodule of `N₀`. Therefore, `P ≤ sSup (B N₀ hN₀)`.

Since `P ≤ sSup (B N₀ hN₀)`, and `sSup (B N₀ hN₀)` is one of the terms in the union, we have `P ≤ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN)`. This proves the first part.

that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · sorry
  sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
    exact h₁.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ∀ A : I, ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← hf' A.prop]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ≤ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ≤ A ∧ N.val ≤ B → A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ⟨h_le, h_neq⟩
  have h_neq' : A.val ≠ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ≤ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ⟨h_mem⟩
      · exact (h_mem ▸ A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ≤ A.val ⊓ B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ≤ (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ▸ N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ]

We need to complete the proof of `refinement`. The structure of `refinement` is:
- `carrier := ⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN`
- `sSupIndep' := by sorry`
- `sSup_eq_top' := by sorry`
- `not_bot_mem' := by sorry`

Let's complete the proof for `sSup_eq_top'`. The goal is to show that the `sSup` of the union is `⊤`.

```tactics
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · sorry
  sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
    exact h₁.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ∀ A : I, ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← hf' A.prop]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N ≤ A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val ≤ A ∧ N.val ≤ B → A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with ⟨h_le, h_neq⟩
  have h_neq' : A.val ≠ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s ≤ I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with ⟨h_mem⟩
      · exact (h_mem ▸ A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val ≤ A.val ⊓ B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N ≤ (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot ▸ N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J → I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' ▸ N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N₁ N₂ : J) (h_le₁ : N₂.val ≤ RefinementMap I J h N₁) :
    RefinementMap I J h N₂ = RefinementMap I J h N₁ := by
  have h_le₂ : N₂.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N₂) := RefinementMapLE I J h N₂
  exact (UniqueGE I J N₂ (RefinementMap I J h N₁) (RefinementMap I J h N₂) (⟨h_le₁, h_le₂⟩)).symm

/-- If `N ≤ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ≤ A → (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (⟨h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N⟩)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ≤ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D₁ ≤ D₂` if

ementMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, N.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  · simp [RefinementMapId]
  · intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N₁ N₂ : J) (h_le₁ : N₂.val ≤ RefinementMap I J h N₁) :
    RefinementMap I J h N₂ = RefinementMap I J h N₁ := by
  have h_le₂ : N₂.val ≤ (RefinementMap I J h N₂) := RefinementMapLE I J h N₂
  exact (UniqueGE I J N₂ (RefinementMap I J h N₁) (RefinementMap I J h N₂) (⟨h_le₁, h_le₂⟩)).symm

/-- If `N ≤ A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val ≤ A → (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (⟨h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h N⟩)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val ≤ RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D₁ ≤ D₂` if
and only if `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le D₁ D₂ := IsRefinement D₁ D₂
  --D₁ ≤ D₂ iff D₁ refines D₂.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans I₁ I₂ I₃ h12 h23 := by
    intro N h_mem
    rcases (h23 h_mem) with ⟨C, h_sub, h_eq⟩
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := ⨆ (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))
    use A
    constructor
    · apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c
      exact hf (h_sub c.prop)
    · have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor
        · intro a h_a
          have h_aux'' : ∃ (c : C), a ∈ (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop
          rcases h_aux'' with ⟨c_a, h_ca⟩
          have h_le : a ≤ c_a := by rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := ⟨c, h_mem_c⟩
        have h_le_c : c ≤ sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by
          rw [← hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a ∈ A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) ≤ A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ∀ N ∈ J, N ∈ I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with ⟨h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_I⟩
      let N' : J := ⟨N, h_N_in_J⟩
      have h_A : ∃ A : I, N ≤ A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with ⟨A, h_N_le_A⟩
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let h_B₁ := hf' N'.prop
      let h_B₂ := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_B₁
      have h_mem : A.val ∈ f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_B₂ h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) ≤ A.val := h_B₁ ▸ h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [← h_B₁] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ≤ N := by
        simpa [← h_B₁] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N ∈ I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N ▸ A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ∀ N ∈ I, N ∈ J := by
      sorry
    aesop

variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `S` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D`. -/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def refinement (B : ∀ N ∈ D, Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))
    (hB : ∀ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN)) (hB' : ∀ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))
    (hB'' : ∀ N hN, ⊥ ∉ B N hN) : DirectSumDecomposition M where
  carrier := ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry

import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · sorry
  sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
    exact h₁.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩

: N ∈ D), B N hN
  sSupIndep' x hx a ha ha' := by
    sorry
  sSup_eq_top' := by
    sorry
  not_bot_mem' := by
    sorry
```

import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ D₂ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · sorry
  sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp [B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ∀ A : I, ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← hf' A.prop]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h
